title: Initialize
description: Method `initialize` (host â†’ ext, request). The handshake: the host announces its `protocol_version`, identity, workspace + trust, session, frontend `mode`/UI capabilities, and which capability groups are enabled; the extension replies with the negotiated `protocol_version`, its identity, and its registrations (tools, commands, flags, event subscriptions). Both sides ignore unknown fields; the effective protocol version is `min(host, ext)`.

protocol_version.description: Highest SEP version the host speaks.

root.description: Absolute path to the active workspace root.

trusted.description: Whether the workspace is trusted. Project extensions load only when true.

session.description: Present when the host has a session concept (daemon/CLI); absent for the bare engine.

mode.description: Frontend rendering mode; drives which `ui/request` kinds are answerable.

ui_capabilities.description: UI request kinds the frontend can render, e.g. `select`, `confirm`, `input`, `notify`, `set_status`, `set_widget`, `set_title`. Empty â‡’ headless.

flags.description: Parsed values for the flags the extension declares (name â†’ value). A host with a CLI surface fills this; hosts without one send it empty or omit it. The extension reads its flag values here at startup.

capabilities_enabled.description: Which host capability groups the extension may call. A method in a disabled group is rejected with -32004 CapabilityDisabled.

protocol_version.description: Highest SEP version the extension speaks. Effective version = min(host, ext).

Registrations.title: SepRegistrations
Registrations.description: What the extension contributes. Also the payload of a later `registry/update` notification.

flags.description: CLI/slash flag names the extension owns; the host delivers their parsed values in `initialize` params `flags`.

shortcuts.description: Keyboard shortcuts binding a chord to a registered command. Only frontends with a key surface (the TUI) honor them.

subscriptions.description: Event names the extension wants delivered. Doubles as the host's dispatch filter â€” unsubscribed events are never sent.

providers.description: LLM providers the extension contributes to the host's model surface (Phase 7). Proxied via `provider/complete`.

hooks.description: Intercept hooks the extension handles (Phase 8). Unlike `subscriptions` (observe events), hooks are awaited requests the host drives every turn â€” declaring them lets the host skip the per-turn `context` hook when nobody handles it. Empty/absent â‡’ unknown, so the host runs every hook to be safe (a pre-Phase-8 extension that hooks `tool_call` still fires).

message_renderers.description: Declarative custom-message renderers (Phase 8): a message `tag` â†’ render-block template. Data-only; the frontend renders.

tag.description: Custom message tag this renderer claims (e.g. `snake_board`).

template.description: Render-block template; string fields may contain `{{path}}` placeholders resolved against the message's data.

RenderBlock.title: SepRenderBlock
RenderBlock.description: The declarative render-block DSL (Phase 8) â€” replaces pi's function renderers. Frontends render each `kind` natively (TUI/web/widget); `text` is the always-available plain fallback (a frontend may derive one when omitted). The `widget` kind is the interactive tier: it wraps a `body` block and declares `keybindings`; the host routes matching keys back as `widget/key` events and the extension re-renders via `ui/request` `set_widget`.

name.description: Provider name, unique within the host's merged model surface.

base_url.description: Informational upstream base URL (the extension does the real call). Omit to keep it private.

api_key_env.description: Env var the extension reads its API key from â€” informational to the host. Omit for OAuth-only providers.

id.description: Model id the host passes back in `provider/complete`.

name.description: Bare tool name; the host exposes it as `<extension>.<name>`.

parameters.description: JSON Schema for the tool's arguments.

deferred.description: If true, register into the host's deferred (tool_search-promoted) tier instead of eager.

key.description: Human-typed chord, e.g. `ctrl+p` or `f2`; the frontend parses it.

command.description: The registered command name this chord invokes (no leading `/`).
